 {"id":"gt-rgd9x","title":"Deprecate hook files, use pinned beads for propulsion","description":"## Problem

Hook files were "deprecated" via comments but NOT eradicated. The code is all still there and actively used.

## Current State (NOT Done)

**Live hook file exists:**
- `/Users/stevey/gt/deacon/.beads/hook-deacon.json`

**Code still calling hook functions:**
- `resume.go:151` - WriteSlungWork
- `park.go:114` - ReadHook  
- `spawn.go:407` - WriteSlungWork
- `molecule_step.go:293` - WriteSlungWork
- `molecule_step.go:368` - BurnHook

**Hook infrastructure still exists:**
- `internal/wisp/io.go` - All hook I/O functions
- `internal/wisp/types.go` - SlungWork, Wisp types, constants
- `.beads/.gitignore` - hook-*.json pattern

**14 docs still reference hooks:**
architecture.md, beads-data-plane.md, molecular-chemistry.md, molecules.md,
pinned-beads-design.md, polecat-lifecycle.md, polecat-wisp-architecture.md,
propulsion-principle.md, session-lifecycle.md, sling-design.md, vision.md,
wisp-architecture.md, witness-patrol-design.md, ~/gt/CLAUDE.md

## Goal: Total Eradication

1. Remove ALL hook file code from callers
2. Delete hook functions from internal/wisp/
3. Delete hook types and constants
4. Remove gitignore patterns
5. Delete any live hook files, convert to pinned beads
6. Update all documentation
7. Verify propulsion cycle works with pinned beads only","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"gastown/crew/max","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:36:31.396028-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T16:46:02.720418-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T16:46:02.720418-08:00","close_reason":"Hook files eradicated: code removed, types removed, live files deleted, gitignore cleaned"}

 {"id":"gt-u818","title":"Witness Plugin System","description":"Patrol extensions via molecule bonding.

## Core Insight

Plugins ARE molecules. No separate format needed.

## How It Works

```
mol-witness-patrol
    │
    ├── survey-workers
    │
    ├── plugin-run ←── bonds registered plugin molecules
    │       │
    │       ├── mol-mood-check (if registered)
    │       ├── mol-security-scan (if registered)
    │       └── mol-custom-X (user-installed)
    │
    └── aggregate (WaitsFor: all-children)
```

## Plugin Molecule Structure

A plugin is just a molecule proto with specific labels:

```json
{
  "id": "mol-mood-check",
  "title": "Polecat Mood Check",
  "description": "Assess polecat emotional state from output. Vars: {{polecat_name}}, {{captured_output}}",
  "labels": ["template", "plugin", "witness", "tier:haiku"],
  "issue_type": "task"
}
```

Labels encode metadata:
- `plugin` - marks as bondable plugin
- `witness` / `deacon` / `refinery` - which patrol can use it
- `tier:haiku` / `tier:sonnet` - model tier hint

## Registration

Plugins registered in rig config or molecules.jsonl:

```bash
# Install from Mol Mall
bd mol install mol-mood-check

# Or add to ~/.beads/molecules.jsonl manually
```

## Execution

The `plugin-run` step in patrol:

```bash
# For each registered plugin molecule matching this patrol:
bd mol bond mol-mood-check $PATROL_WISP \
  --ref mood-{{polecat_name}} \
  --var polecat_name=$POLECAT \
  --var captured_output="$OUTPUT"
```

## CLI

- `bd mol list --label plugin` - List available plugins
- `bd mol install <id>` - Install from Mol Mall
- `gt patrol plugins <role>` - Show plugins for a patrol role

## Benefits

- No YAML schema to maintain
- No separate plugin loading code
- Plugins are versioned, synced, auditable (they are beads)
- Distribution via Mol Mall (molecules.jsonl registry)
- Same {{var}} substitution as all molecules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:16:53.886931-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:00.654095-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:00.654095-08:00","close_reason":"Future features. Witness plugins, code review molecule, and Mol Mall are post-v0.1."}
 {"id":"gt-u82","title":"Design: Mayor session cycling and handoff","description":"Design for Mayor session cycling and structured handoff.

## Overview

Mayor coordinates across all rigs and runs for extended periods. Needs session cycling pattern with structured handoff notes.

## Key Elements

1. Session cycling recognition (when to cycle)
2. Handoff note format (structured state capture)
3. Handoff delivery (mail to self)
4. Fresh session startup (reading and resuming)

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-g2d: Mayor session cycling prompting
- gt-sye: Mayor startup protocol prompting
- gt-vci: Mayor handoff mail template
- gt-1le: town handoff command (optional, P2)

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:03:16.125725-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"epic"}
